bids.reasoning: At t=0.00 there is no time pressure to concede, so I am targeting a near-maximum utility bid for myself. The opponent’s opening offer gives me low utility and their urgency message is not supported by the actual time fraction, so I will counter firmly with my preferred values across the key issues.

At t=0.02 it is still very early, so I am targeting a very high-utility bid while making only a small concession to avoid simply repeating my prior offer. The opponent’s offers are still very unfavorable to my preferences and have not improved; their urgency messages are cheap talk given the actual early time. I concede only on low-cost issueE, moving from my top valueB to nearly equivalent valueA.

At t=0.04 it is still very early, so I am targeting a near-maximum utility bid rather than making major concessions. The opponent’s offers have only slightly improved for me and remain low-utility, while their urgency messages are not supported by the actual time pressure. I will avoid repeating my immediately previous bid by returning to my strongest package.

I am targeting a near-max utility bid because it is still very early (t=0.06), and the opponent’s offers remain low-value for me with only minor concessions. Their urgency messages look like cheap-talk pressure rather than evidence of real deadline risk, so I will make only a small concession on the low-weight issueE while holding firm on my most important issues.

I am targeting a near-maximum utility bid because it is still very early at t=0.08, and there is no real deadline pressure despite the opponent’s urgency messages. The opponent’s offers remain very low utility for me and do not show meaningful concession, so I will hold firm on my high-weight issues while avoiding the exact same bid as my immediately previous offer.

At t=0.10 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making only a small concession on lower-weight issues. The opponent’s offers remain very low for my utility and their urgency messages are not supported by the actual time fraction, but they have repeatedly pushed issueD/issueE away from my top choices, so I am slightly relaxing those while holding firm on high-weight issueA, issueB, and issueC.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.83 while making a small concession on a low-weight issue, since it is still early at t=0.12 and there is no need to accept the opponent’s fake urgency. The opponent’s latest offer improved on issueC by offering my top value there, but remains very poor for me on issueA and issueB, so I will hold firm on those high-importance issues while conceding issueD toward a value they have repeatedly proposed.

At t=0.14 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making a small concession on a low-weight issue rather than moving on issueA, issueB, or issueC. The opponent’s offers remain low for me and their urgency messages are not supported by the actual time fraction, but they repeatedly propose issueD=valueE, so I can concede there while holding my important issues.

At t=0.16 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid for myself while making limited concessions on lower-weight issues. The opponent’s offers remain very low for my utility and their urgency messages are not credible given the actual early time, but they have shown interest in valueE/D on lower-weight issues, so I concede issueD while holding firm on issueA, issueB, and issueC.

I am targeting a still-high utility bid around 0.77, since we are only at t=0.18 and the opponent’s offers remain quite unfavorable to me despite minor movement on issueC. The opponent’s repeated urgency messages are not supported by the actual time remaining, but their offers suggest they may value issueD/issueE as valueE, so I concede there while holding firm on my highest-weight issues issueA and issueB.

I am targeting a still-strong utility bid around the mid-0.75 range, since t=0.20 is early enough to hold firm on my high-weight issues. The opponent’s offers remain very poor for me and their urgency messages are not credible given the actual time, but they have signaled interest in low-value concessions on issueD/issueE, so I concede there while protecting issueA, issueB, and issueC.

At t=0.22 it is still early, so I am targeting a strong utility bid while making concessions mainly on the lower-weight issuesD and issueE. The opponent’s offers and messages show persistent pressure and little real concession, with repeated preference signals around valueE on several issues, so I will hold firm on my high-weight issues A, B, and C while testing movement on lower-cost dimensions.

I am targeting a strong utility around 0.754 by holding firm on the high-importance issues A, B, and C while conceding on lower-weight issues D and E. At t=0.24 there is still no need for major concessions, and the opponent’s repeated urgency messages are not credible given the actual time remaining. Their offers show some movement on issueC toward my preferred valueB, but they remain very tough on issueA and issueB, so I am offering concessions mainly where they cost me least.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.74 by holding firm on my highest-value issues A, B, and C while conceding the lower-weight D and E toward values the opponent has repeatedly proposed. At t=0.26 there is no need to make a major concession despite the opponent’s repeated urgency messages, which are not supported by the actual time remaining. Their offers show they prefer low-utility values for me on B/D/E especially, so I am testing movement on D and E first.

I am targeting a utility around 0.75: still protecting my highest-weight issues issueA, issueB, and issueC, while conceding on the lower-weight issueD and issueE. The opponent’s repeated urgency messages are cheap talk at t=0.28, but their latest offer finally moved to my top value on issueA, so I will build on that signal without conceding too much too early.

I am targeting a high but not maximal utility around 0.77 because we are only at t=0.30, so there is no need to accept the opponent’s low-utility offers yet. The opponent’s messages are mostly generic urgency pressure, while their bids show limited concession; however, they have once offered issueA:valueA and sometimes moved on issueC, so I will hold my high-weight issues and concede mainly on lower-weight issueD/issueE.

I am targeting a solid mid-game utility around 0.70 by holding firm on my most important issueA and best issueC, while conceding on lower-weight issueD/issueE and partially on issueB. The opponent’s offers have improved for me recently, especially by offering issueB:valueB and issueC:valueB, but their repeated urgency messages are cheap talk at t=0.32, so I will make a meaningful but not deadline-level concession.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.686, conceding mainly on lower-weight issueD/issueE and moderately on issueB while holding my highest-value positions on issueA and issueC. At t=0.34 there is some time pressure but not enough to accept the opponent’s low-utility framing; their messages repeatedly claim urgency, but the actual time and offers show gradual concessions rather than a true deadline crisis.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.67 because we are only at t=0.36, so I should concede but not collapse toward the opponent’s latest offer. The opponent’s bids have improved somewhat for me, especially on issueA and issueC, but their repeated urgency messages are cheap talk and not consistent with the actual early/mid negotiation time. I am conceding mainly on lower-weight issueD/issueE and moderately on issueB while holding firm on my highest-value issueA and issueC.

I targeted a mid-high utility bid around 0.69 while conceding on lower-weight issues D and E to match the opponent’s recent signals. At t=0.38 there is still time, but the opponent has meaningfully improved on issueA and issueC while remaining tough on issueB, so I hold issueB at valueB rather than dropping to their valueE. Their urgency messages are repetitive cheap talk and not consistent with the actual time remaining.

At t=0.40 I am targeting a strong but more negotiable bid around 0.72 utility, since there is still time and the opponent’s latest offer is quite low for me despite their repeated urgency messages. Their offers show some willingness to give issueA=valueA and issueC=valueB, while they seem to care more about concessions on issueD/issueE and sometimes issueB, so I am holding firm on my most important issues and conceding selectively elsewhere.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.71: still protecting my highest-weight issueA and issueC, while conceding on lower-weight issueD and issueE and partially on issueB. At t=0.42 there is some time pressure but not enough to accept the opponent’s low-utility offers; their actual bids show movement on some issues but remain weak for me despite repeated urgency messages.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.70 while making a visible mid-game concession on lower-weight issues D and E, both of which the opponent has repeatedly proposed as valueE. The opponent’s offers have improved somewhat on issueA and issueB but still ask for a major concession on issueC, so I am holding issueC at my best valueB and keeping issueA at valueA due to its high importance. Their repeated urgency messages are cheap talk at t=0.44, so I am responding to the actual bidding pattern rather than the pressure language.
